abc1234 Add retry logic to upstream sync job (2 hours ago) <Alice Quant>
This change introduces an exponential backoff retry policy for the
upstream sync job. Previously, transient 5xx responses from the
provider would cause the whole pipeline to abort and require a manual
restart, which was eating into our oncall budget.

The retry is capped at 5 attempts with a 30s ceiling per wait.
Anything beyond that should be treated as a hard failure and paged.

We also added a small jitter term to avoid the classic thundering
herd if the provider blips and all our shards retry in lockstep.

Closes #481.

Signed-off-by: Alice Quant <alice@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Bob Builder <bob@example.com>
---END---
def5678 Fix off-by-one in window aggregation (yesterday) <Bob Builder>
The bucket boundary was being computed as floor(t / w) * w, but the
left edge of the bucket should be exclusive when t lands exactly on
the boundary. This caused a one-bucket lag in the 1-minute window
that surfaced during the post-mortem of the April 3 incident.

Switched to ceil((t + 1) / w) * w - w so the edges line up with how
downstream consumers slice the series.

Added a regression test fixture from the recorded production trace.

Signed-off-by: Bob Builder <bob@example.com>
---END---
9988aaa Bump build pins (3 days ago) <Cynthia Lee>
Routine dependency refresh — no behaviour change.
---END---
fed4321 Drop deprecated --legacy-format flag (4 days ago) <Dan Refactor>
The --legacy-format flag has been emitting a warning since 0.18 and
shipped in deprecation mode for two minor versions. Removing it now
so we can stop carrying the compatibility shim in the parser.

If anyone is still relying on it, the suggested replacement is
--format=legacy.json which produces identical output structure.

Signed-off-by: Dan Refactor <dan@example.com>
---END---
0123456 Tighten input validation on /ingest endpoint (5 days ago) <Eve Security>
Reject payloads exceeding 4 MiB at the edge rather than at the worker
to avoid the memory spike we saw during the synthetic load test on
Tuesday. The worker still keeps its own check as a defence-in-depth
backstop.

Returns 413 with a structured error body so clients can distinguish
"too large" from "malformed".

Signed-off-by: Eve Security <eve@example.com>
---END---
